channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Starmer on the line as UK holds "most important local election in decades"
item.description: Keir Starmer isn’t on the ballot, but the U.K. prime minister’s future is on the line in a special election on June 18. Voters in the Makerfield district of northwest England are electing a new lawmaker. The leading contender is Andy Burnham, the current Labour mayor of Greater Manchester and the favourite to be the next prime minister. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot explains why.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US tells NATO it will review its force presence in Europe
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told NATO the Pentagon will conduct a review of its force presence in Europe within the next six months. The review will depend on how fast European NATO allies take responsibility for their own security. This comes on the back of a G7 summit deemed a success, as Trump has signalled backing Ukraine against Russia. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating tells us more from Brussels.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Long Island serial killer sentenced to life in prison
item.description: A chilling mystery that haunted New York for more than a decade has finally reached a definitive end. On June 17, a Long Island architect was sentenced to life in prison without parole for a horrifying string of serial killings. The "Gilgo Killer" targeted young women mostly in their 20s, who worked as online escorts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New US Fed chair vows reforms as central bank signals rate hikes on horizon
item.description: As widely expected, policymakers at the the US Federal Reserve have kept interest rates unchanged. But in a clear change of tone from previous meetings, Fed officials said they expected raising interest rates later this year rather than lowering them, to tame rising inflation. The meeting was the first chaired by the central bank's new chief, Kevin Warsh, who scrapped guidance about future rate moves and announced new task forces that could reshape how the Fed communicates and analyses data.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mixed reactions over US-Iran deal in Lebanon
item.description: Although the US-Iran deal brings hope to Lebanon, reactions remain mixed. Lebanon may be mentioned in the first paragraph, it is not included in the $300 million reconstruction fund despite the south of the country having been totally destroyed, and Israeli strikes have not stopped. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more about it from Beirut.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran deal "worst policy blunder in decades": criticism spreads across US political spectrum
item.description: US President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart, President Masoud Pezeshkian, have both now signed the memorandum of understanding, designed to bring the conflict in the middle east to a close. The document, which consists of 14 points, has not yet been released, but officials have clarified much of what's in it. The memorandum has been met with heavy criticism back in the US, from both Democrats and Republicans. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert explains why.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Is football coming home? English fans tell FRANCE 24
item.description: Can Team England bring the #FIFAWorldCup home, 60 years after its first did so in 1966?FRANCE 24 asks some supporters of the #ThreeLions what they think.Do you think #football is coming home? Let us know in the comments below!
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At the mercy of Silicon Valley? Europe exposed by Trump AI export ban
item.description: Is it better to persuade or to confront? French leader Emmanuel Macron is opting for the royal treatment with Donald Trump at Versailles, what with a dinner to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the King of France's support for the birth of a new nation. It's all about keeping the US president on board: on Ukraine, reopening the Strait of Hormuz and more. But will flattery work when it comes to the AI revolution?
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Explosions and gunfire heard at airport in Niger's capital Niamey
item.description: Gunfire and explosions were heard at the airport in Niger's capital Niamey early Thursday, according to witnesses, and security forces had blocked off the area, residents said.
item.source: © Planet Labs PBC handout via AFP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with 'large-scale' attack, oil refinery strike 'justified', Zelensky says
item.description: Smoke rose over Moscow Thursday morning after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack in years, hitting the Moscow oil refinery and sparking fires in and around the capital. The attack on the oil refinery was a "justified response" to Russian strikes in Ukraine, said President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Starmer rival Andy Burnham seeks win in local UK poll pivotal to PM's fate
item.description: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is hoping to emerge triumphant from a key local election Thursday that could propel the popular Labour party veteran to the UK premiership. Several figures within the centre-left party are positioning themselves to challenge the deeply unpopular Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Tehran and Washington sign interim peace deal to end US-Israeli war on Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed an interim deal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran. The two countries now have 60 days to hammer out a lasting agreement that would see Tehran diluting its enriched uranium stocks in exchange for sweeping sanctions relief and a $300 billion reconstruction fund.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: England outshine Croatia in thriller, Portugal held to draw
item.description: England made a winning start to their World Cup campaign as they overpowered Croatia in a thriller in Dallas (4-2). Harry Kane scored twice, bringing him level with Gary Lineker on 10 World Cup goals.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: G7 hails unity on Russia as Trump signals tougher line on Moscow
item.description: G7 leaders on Wednesday welcomed growing unity in efforts to increase pressure on Russia to end the war in Ukraine, amid signs of President Donald Trump's willingness to take a tougher line on Moscow. Later, at the candlelit dinner in Versailles, Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Lula warns Trump against meddling in Brazil election after criticism of judiciary
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Wednesday warned President Donald Trump against interfering in Brazil's October presidential election after renewed US criticism of judicial actions against Lula's political opponents. The exchange underscores rising tensions as Washington pushes new tariffs and labels two drug-trafficking groups as foreign terrorist organisations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ghana break Panama resistance with stoppage-time winner
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ghana break Panama resistance with stoppage-time winner
item.description: Ghana secured a dramatic 1-0 victory over Panama thanks to a stoppage-time strike from Caleb Yirenkyi. The Black Stars were frustrated for long spells by a disciplined Panamanian side but found a breakthrough deep into added time to claim all three points.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Raul Castro backs sweeping Cuba reforms to revive struggling economy
item.description: Cuba's former president Raul Castro on Wednesday backed sweeping economic reforms proposed by the government to revive the crisis-hit economy amid a longstanding US embargo. The measures would expand private investment, attract capital from Cubans abroad and reduce the state's role, with lawmakers expected to approve key changes this week.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Oil prices fall more than 3% after Trump signs deal reopening Hormuz Strait
item.description: Oil prices fell more than 3% Thursday after US President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian signed off on a deal to end the Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas exports flowed through the waterway before Tehran effectively closed it off after the war began. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump has signed Iran deal, says US official
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Kane, Bellingham and Rashford fire England past Croatia
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Kane, Bellingham and Rashford fire England past Croatia
item.description: England claimed a convincing 4-2 victory over Croatia in an enthralling Group L encounter in Arlington, Texas, with Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford combining to overcome a resilient Croatian comeback. The Three Lions survived sustained pressure from Zlatko Dalic's side in a match that lived up to its billing as a rematch of the 2018 World Cup semi-final.
item.source: © François Nel, Getty Images via AFP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: A World Cup of exclusion? US immigration policies weigh on FIFA tournament
item.description: The Iranian football team have this week criticised their treatment in the US, with the team's head coach saying they're being "oppressed" at the World Cup. Iran – one of four countries that have been hit by a travel ban – have faced restrictions, visa complications and logistical disruptions linked to the war in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Dr Lindsay Krasnoff, Clinical Assistant Professor at NYU's Tisch Institute for Global Sport.
item.dc:creator: Sharon GAFFNEY
